secondary_meaning: interconnected system or group of people
usage_notes: Common in technology and social contexts; feminine forms: une réseau when referring to specific networks
common_collocations: réseau WiFi, réseau social, réseau d'amis
image_context: Connected network nodes representing social or computer network
example_1: Le réseau WiFi est très lent.
example_1_translation: The WiFi network is very slow.
example_2: Il a un grand réseau d'amis.
example_2_translation: He has a large network of friends.
example_3: Les réseaux sociaux sont importants aujourd'hui.
pronunciation_tips: Rhymes with 'eau'. Pronounce the 'eau' like 'o' in 'go'.
memory_hook: Network sounds like réseau - think connected nodes.

secondary_meaning: the act of applying or using something
usage_notes: Used both for software apps and abstract applications. Always feminine.
common_collocations: télécharger une application, application mobile, application de bureau
image_context: Smartphone with various app icons on screen
example_3: Cette application est très utile pour apprendre.
example_3_translation: This application is very useful for learning.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on last syllable: appli-ca-SION. The 'tion' sounds like 'syohn'.
memory_hook: App + lication = application. A shortened 'app' is from application.
common_mistakes: Do not use 'applicateur' for the app itself

usage_notes: Universal in office and academic contexts. Always masculine.
common_collocations: document important, document PDF, document Word
image_context: Word document icon on computer screen
example_2_translation: Send me the document by email.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on first syllable: DOC-u-ment. The 'u' is like 'oo' in 'book'.

secondary_meaning: a remark or observation expressing an opinion
usage_notes: Common on social media and forums. Always masculine.
common_collocations: laisser un commentaire, commentaire positif, commentaires fermés
image_context: Social media post with comment bubbles
example_1: Il a écrit un commentaire sous l'article.
example_1_translation: He wrote a comment under the article.
example_3_translation: Thank you for your helpful comment.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on last: com-man-TAIRE. The 'taire' rhymes with 'pair'.
memory_hook: Comment + aire = commentaire. Comment on something.
common_mistakes: Do not confuse with 'comment' (how); 'commentaire' is the noun form

usage_notes: Used as adjective before nouns. Can also function as noun (someone in advertising).
common_collocations: spot publicitaire, agence publicitaire, message publicitaire
example_1: Le spot publicitaire est trop long.
example_1_translation: The advertising spot is too long.
example_2: Elle travaille dans une agence publicitaire.
example_2_translation: She works in an advertising agency.
example_3: Cette campagne publicitaire est très créative.
example_3_translation: This advertising campaign is very creative.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on '-taire' at end: pub-lis-i-TAIRE. The 'aire' rhymes with 'pair'.
memory_hook: Publicité (advertising) + aire = publicitaire (adj relating to ads).
common_mistakes: Do not confuse with 'publicain' (tax collector, biblical term)

secondary_meaning: a person or entity intended to receive something
usage_notes: Can be masculine or feminine depending on the person. Used for mail, messages, etc.
common_collocations: destinataire du colis, adresse du destinataire, destinataire inconnu
image_context: Envelope with address written ready to be mailed
example_1: Le destinataire n'a pas reçu le colis.
example_1_translation: The recipient didn't receive the package.
example_3: Le destinataire était absent à la livraison.
example_3_translation: The recipient was absent at delivery.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on last: des-ti-na-TAIRE. The 'taire' rhymes with 'pair'.
memory_hook: Destiné (destined) + ataire = destinataire (the person destined to receive).
common_mistakes: Do not confuse with 'destiné' (destined); 'destinataire' is a person.

secondary_meaning: a bank note or piece of paper giving access to an event
usage_notes: Universal for travel and events. Always masculine.
common_collocations: billet d'avion, billet de train, billet de cinéma
image_context: Airline ticket with flight details displayed
example_2_translation: The ticket costs one hundred euros.
example_3_translation: Do you have your return ticket?
memory_hook: Billet = bill + et. A piece of paper like a bill/ticket.
common_mistakes: Do not confuse with 'billette' (smaller piece) or 'bilan' (balance sheet)

secondary_meaning: a planned route or journey plan
usage_notes: Essential for travel planning. Always masculine.
common_collocations: itinéraire de voyage, suivre l'itinéraire, itinéraire touristique
image_context: Map with marked travel route between cities
example_1: Nous avons préparé notre itinéraire de voyage.
example_2_translation: The route passes through several cities.
example_3_translation: Can you recommend a scenic itinerary?
pronunciation_tips: Stress on first: I-ti-né-raire. The 'ai' sounds like 'eh'.
memory_hook: Itinerary + French ending = itinéraire. Think 'itinerary' for travel planning.
common_mistakes: Do not write 'itinénaire' (though you'll see it sometimes in French)

secondary_meaning: useful or suitable for actual use
usage_notes: Common adjective describing objects or solutions that are useful.
common_collocations: c'est pratique, très pratique, solution pratique
image_context: Organized toolbox with practical organization system
example_3: Les chaussures pratiques sont meilleures pour marcher.
example_3_translation: Practical shoes are better for walking.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on first: PRAK-tik. The 'a' sounds like 'ah' in 'cat'.
memory_hook: Practical sounds like 'pratique'. Same meaning in both languages!
common_mistakes: Do not confuse with 'pratiquant' (someone who practices a religion)

usage_notes: Enthusiastic adjective for describing beautiful things or people.
common_collocations: c'est magnifique, très magnifique, paysage magnifique
image_context: Magnificent mountain landscape at sunset with golden light
pronunciation_tips: Stress on last: ma-gni-FIK. The 'fi' sounds like 'fee'.
memory_hook: Magnifique sounds like 'magnificent'. Beautiful in both languages!

secondary_meaning: giving careful consideration, sincere, or not joking
usage_notes: Describes a serious attitude, problem, or person. Changes form with gender.
common_collocations: c'est sérieux, très sérieux, problème sérieux, personne sérieuse
image_context: Professional serious businessman in formal suit
example_2: Il est très sérieux dans son travail.
example_2_translation: He is very serious in his work.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on first: SÉ-ri-eux. The 'eu' sounds like 'uh'.
memory_hook: Sérieux = serious in French. Think of 'serio' (Spanish for serious).

secondary_meaning: in the present time; at this very moment
common_collocations: actuellement habiter, travailler actuellement, actuellement en cours
image_context: Clock showing current time with calendar
example_2: Il travaille actuellement sur un projet.
example_2_translation: He's currently working on a project.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on last: ak-tu-EL-ment. The 'uel' sounds like 'well'.
common_mistakes: Do NOT translate as 'actually'. Use 'en fait' or 'réellement' for actual.

secondary_meaning: at any future time; starting from this moment forward
usage_notes: More formal than 'désormais'. Used for commitments and rules.
common_collocations: dorénavant, je vais, dorénavant, nous allons
image_context: Road sign pointing forward with sunrise background
example_1_translation: From now on, I take the bus.
example_2_translation: Let's be more careful from now on.
example_3_translation: From now on, you must study more.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on first: DO-ré-na-vant. Rhymes with French 'avant' (before).
memory_hook: Dore + navant (like 'avant' = before) = dorénavant (from before now = from now on).
common_mistakes: Do not confuse with 'de nos jours' (nowadays)

secondary_meaning: identical or similar in every way
usage_notes: Can also be used as 'c'est pareil' (it's the same). Changes with gender/number.
common_collocations: c'est pareil, pareil que, pareil à
image_context: Two identical objects side by side comparison
example_1: Nous avons le même problème, c'est pareil.
example_1_translation: We have the same problem, it's alike.
example_2: Les deux maisons se ressemblent, elles sont pareilles.
example_2_translation: The two houses look alike, they are the same.
memory_hook: Pareil = pair (pair, couple) + similar = the same as its pair.
common_mistakes: Do not confuse with 'parole' (word) or 'parure' (set/matching pair)

secondary_meaning: not the same as another; distinct or unlike
usage_notes: Common for comparisons. Changes with gender/number.
common_collocations: très différent, différent de, différents pays
image_context: Two contrasting colored objects showing difference
example_2: Ce travail est différent de l'autre.
example_2_translation: This work is different from the other.
example_3: Les deux sœurs sont très différentes.
example_3_translation: The two sisters are very different.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on first: DI-fé-rant. The 'é' sounds like 'ay'.
memory_hook: Différent = different. Same word + pronunciation in both languages!
